'''Dallas Hood''' (born 11 December 1977) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level for [[Sydney Roosters]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}}.

==Playing career==
Hood made his debut for Sydney in 1997.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|title=Men's Honour Roll|website=Sydney Roosters|access-date=23 January 2019|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327090103/https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|url-status=dead}}</ref> Hood appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in the [[Sydney Roosters]] 6–14 defeat by the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2000 NRL Grand Final]] at [[Stadium Australia]] on Sunday 27 August 2000.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2000/grand-final/brisbane-vs-sydney/summary.html |title=NRL 2000 - Grand Final - Brisbane Broncos 14 def. Sydney Roosters 6 |website=Rugbyleagueproject.org |access-date=22 February 2024}}</ref> In August 2002, Hood signed a two-year deal to join Wakefield Trinity Wildcats.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2221651.stm |title=Wildcats turn to Hood |work=BBC Sport |date=22 August 2002 |access-date=22 February 2024}}</ref>
